_README: OPTIMISTIC critical-path plan for the NR4A3 PROTAC-degrader paper (ChemRxiv preprint + JCIM).
This file is the SINGLE SOURCE the daily status email projects into a day-by-day calendar.
It is MAINTAINED BY SESSIONS: as work lands, update each milestone's `status` and, for an
in-progress item, `remaining_days` (what's left, not the original estimate). The email walks the
milestones in dependency order from *today* and assigns optimistic start/finish dates.
Durations are OPTIMISTIC planning estimates ('if all goes to plan'), NOT commitments — GPU spot
capacity waits, failed shards, and red-team findings routinely add days. `status`: done | in_progress | pending.
`track`: gpu | cpu | write | submit.  `parallel_ok`: true = may overlap other gpu legs up to the 8-wide spot quota.
Keep the human-readable rationale for each item in nr4a3-degrader-next-steps.md; this file is just the schedule.
2026-07-15 (trimcrae): TRACK A SHELVED, GO TRACK B. The plan is now trimcrae's three-step spine, preceded by an infra step 0:
(0) step0_rbfe_mechanics - get OpenFE RBFE running on managed-spot GPU (the job running now); prereq for BOTH 1 and 2.
(1) rbfe_congeneric  - verify FEP converges on the KNOWN literature NR4A3 molecule (Zaienne cmpd19); RBFE.
(2) ternary_nrv04    - verify we can replicate the patented NR4A1 degrader's selectivity in-silico (the GATE).
(3) ternary_prospective - design + ternary-test degraders on the cmpd19 anchor; a selective hit is the WIN.
2026-07-15 (trimcrae): steps 1 and 2 are NO LONGER treated as fully parallel. Step 2's quantitative ternary ranking
reuses the same physics-based free-energy machinery step 1 validates, so a step-1 failure invalidates step 2's numbers
too - step 2 now depends_on step 1 as a validity gate (geometry-only prep may overlap; the ternary FE spend waits).
2026-07-15 (trimcrae): ABFE UN-SHELVED - shelving it earlier the same day was a mistake. RBFE gives only RELATIVE
affinity + selectivity; it cannot establish ABSOLUTE NR4A3 binding ('does it bind at all'). So ABFE returns as
Step 1b (abfe_zaienne_anchor: absolute dG on the Zaienne warhead scaffold, anchoring the RBFE series) + Step 1c
(t4l_benchmark: the engine known-answer calibration). Both run in PARALLEL with the RBFE + ternary chain (off the
critical path), so best-case timeline is ~unchanged; they add real GPU $ (expensive - needs a provider/budget nod).

milestones.title: Step 0: get RBFE running on OpenFE + managed-spot GPU (infra shakeout - NOT science yet)
milestones.note: The GPU job running RIGHT NOW. Working out the mechanics of driving OpenFE RBFE on a managed-spot instance (edge 401->lo_m0_NCCO as the shakeout edge). congeneric-RBFE-v3 was failing with ExecuteUserScriptError - re-dispatched ~7x on 2026-07-15 while fixing it. This is a PREREQUISITE for BOTH science steps 1 and 2: neither can produce a valid free-energy result until the OpenFE-on-spot pipeline runs clean. Done = one edge completes end-to-end with a sane dG.

milestones.title: Step 1: does relative FEP CONVERGE self-consistently on the literature-anchored warhead series (Zaienne cmpd19 congeneric map) - method-validity check, NOT a reproduce-the-experiment match
milestones.note: IMPORTANT (do not overstate): cmpd19 has NO measured affinity and NO solved pose (functional target-engagement only), so there is nothing experimental to reproduce. 'Converges' here = SELF-CONSISTENCY of the calculation: forward=reverse hysteresis <=0.5 kcal/mol, adjacent-lambda MBAR overlap, closed-loop cycle closure <=1.0, and the cryptic pocket does NOT collapse during the alchemical MD. It is a PRECISION/does-the-method-behave check on a literature-anchored congeneric series - NOT an accuracy/match-reality check (that would need a public RBFE benchmark set with measured ddG, out of scope). RBFE gives the RELATIVE/selectivity picture; the un-shelved ABFE anchor (abfe_zaienne_anchor) supplies the ABSOLUTE scale - they are COMPLEMENTARY, not either/or (the 2026-07-15 'RBFE not ABFE' framing was a misunderstanding, corrected same day by trimcrae). NB the 401->lo_m0_NCCO edge running now uses OUR de novo molecules (lead-opt comparison + step-0 pipeline shakeout), a SEPARATE edge from the Zaienne convergence pilot. Pilot the most well-behaved edge, then fan out 8-wide. Needs step 0 (working OpenFE-on-spot) first.

milestones.title: Step 1b (UN-SHELVED 2026-07-15): ABSOLUTE binding free energy on the Zaienne anchor - does the warhead bind NR4A3 AT ALL, in silico, and anchor the RBFE series in absolute terms
milestones.note: WHY UN-SHELVED (trimcrae, 2026-07-15): RBFE only gives RELATIVE affinity + selectivity; it can never answer 'does this bind NR4A3 at all'. That absolute number needs an ABFE, and it must be on the SAME scaffold as the warhead series - i.e. on the Zaienne anchor (cmpd19), NOT denovo_401 (different scaffold, cannot anchor the indole RBFE map). Field-standard ABFE: cmpd19 (+ 1-2 well-behaved analogues) x NR4A3 + matched NR4A1/NR4A2 opened conformers, explicit pH-7.4 microstates, forward/reverse convergence + ~3 independent replicates with replicate-SD error bars, plus the lambda-overlap repair (the old NR4A2 soft-core-tail fix folds in here). Cross-checks the published ~9-12 uM functional engagement of cmpd19 and converts every RBFE-connected analogue to an absolute dG. HONEST CAVEATS carried from the shelved work: the openmmtools engine UNDER-BINDS by ~+7.1 kcal/mol (T4L.benzene calibration -> see t4l_benchmark), so absolutes are OFFSET-CORRECTED and one-sided (conservative); the cryptic pocket under-samples reorganization so true binding is likely >= the reported value. EXPENSIVE (multi-leg GPU, ~$80-200 spot est) - needs a provider + budget nod before launch (state provider in advance).

milestones.title: Step 1c (UN-SHELVED 2026-07-15): T4L-L99A ABFE known-answer benchmark - the engine calibration/offset control that makes the absolute numbers defensible
milestones.note: T4-lysozyme L99A + small-ligand (benzene) has a KNOWN experimental dG - the only true reproduce-the-experiment control in the whole plan. Fixes the engine's absolute offset (the ~+7.1 kcal/mol under-binding) so abfe_zaienne_anchor's absolutes can be calibrated. Runs in parallel with the anchor ABFE; combines at the reduce step. Cheap-ish separate small system.

milestones.title: Step 2: replicate the patented NR4A1 degrader's selectivity in-silico - retrospective NR-V04 ternary control (recover NR4A1-degraded / NR4A2.NR4A3-spared)
milestones.note: The GATE on all prospective ranking. Functional retrospective control (Wang 2024 has NO solved ternary structure - not an architecture match). DEPENDS ON STEP 1 as a validity gate: step 2's quantitative selectivity ranking uses the SAME physics-based free-energy machinery step 1 validates, so if step 1 can't reproduce a known binder we can't trust step 2's numbers either (trimcrae, 2026-07-15). Geometry-only parts (ternary pose / interface / Lys presentation) can be prepped in parallel, but the ternary free-energy spend waits on step 1's green light (pilot-one-leg-first). Pilot one paralogue / the epimer functional control first; abort if the known outcome is not recovered.

milestones.title: Step 3: design + ternary-test degraders on the cmpd19 anchor - a selective hit is the WIN (prospective NR4A3 ternary ranking of the matrix)
milestones.note: Only if the NR-V04 control (step 2) passes. Selectivity emerges jointly from warhead x linker x E3 x ternary geometry. Now also consumes the ABSOLUTE binary warhead affinity (from abfe_zaienne_anchor) as one of its per-construct inputs, so each matrix construct carries a real absolute NR4A3 dG, not just a relative one.

milestones.title: Supporting: harmonized pocket-tracking re-analysis (finalize Gate 2 druggability wording in the paper)
milestones.note: Cheap CPU; not a headline step - finalizes the paper's Gate-2 druggability wording.

milestones.title: Fold all results into paper (abstract/S2/S3/S4 + SI) and re-render figures
milestones.note: Single source of truth = nr4a3-degrader-paper.md (+ SI). Now folds in the absolute-affinity result (abfe_zaienne_anchor + t4l_benchmark calibration) alongside the RBFE selectivity + ternary results.

milestones.title: Final red-team + review-response pass (honest-weight every claim; medical-integrity check)
milestones.note: Reviewer-AI block before the outward-facing post/submit step.

milestones.title: Post ChemRxiv (CC-BY) + submit JCIM + send outreach emails
milestones.note: OUTWARD-FACING / irreversible - needs trimcrae sign-off (not auto). This is the finish line.

shelved._reason: ABFE UN-SHELVED 2026-07-15 (trimcrae): shelving ABFE earlier the same day was a MISTAKE based on a misunderstanding - RBFE alone can never establish absolute NR4A3 binding ('does it bind at all'), which the paper needs. ABFE is back as the ABSOLUTE-affinity anchor (abfe_zaienne_anchor) + its engine control (t4l_benchmark), now in the active `milestones` block, retargeted from denovo_401 onto the Zaienne warhead scaffold. RBFE and ABFE are COMPLEMENTARY (relative/selectivity vs absolute), not either/or. Still genuinely shelved: broad DE NOVO warhead GENERATION (distinct from ABFE the method) - that stays parked per the ternary-first strategy.
